item_name: AI Video Generation for Apps (Veo, Runway, Pika, Kling, Luma)
definition_origin: AI video generation refers to text-to-video and image-to-video generative models — Google Veo 3.1, Runway Gen-4/Gen-4.5, Pika 2.2, Kling 3.0, Luma Ray3/Dream Machine. They synthesize 4-10 second clips from prompts (or reference image + prompt). The category emerged in late 2023 (Pika 1.0, Runway Gen-2) and crossed the 'usable for production' threshold by 2026 with Veo 3 (native audio) and Kling 3.0 (native 4K). Solo devs use these to skip the camera-crew/motion-designer line item entirely for short-form app marketing.
core_purpose: Video at the reflective level (Norman) drives store-listing conversion (App Store preview videos lift conversion 25-40%) and at the visceral level supplies launch-day social momentum (Twitter/Threads/TikTok). Functional uses: (1) App Store / Google Play preview video showing app in action (must be real screen capture, not AI — Apple rejects fully-fabricated footage); (2) marketing hero loop on landing page (hero section ambient video, AI-generated establishes mood); (3) onboarding micro-stories (short character clips greeting user); (4) social launch teasers (announcement videos for Twitter/X/Threads/Instagram).
when_needed: Consumer mobile: marketing hero loop (yes), social launch teaser (yes), App Store preview (no — must be screen recording per Apple), in-app onboarding video (rare, prefer Lottie/Rive). B2B/dev-tools: explainer hero video on landing (yes — strong conversion lift), product launch video (yes). AI-wrapper: differentiating launch video (yes — most AI-wrappers skip this and look identical). Marketing: always — short ambient loops are now expected on every modern landing page.
format_options: MP4 H.264 baseline (universal compatibility, ~1-3MB per 8s 1080p clip after compression). MP4 H.265/HEVC (better compression but iOS Safari 13+ only). WebM VP9 (web-optimized, smaller size, no iOS support). MOV ProRes (App Store Connect accepts H.264 or ProRes 422 HQ; ProRes is 100x larger — only for upload, gets transcoded). Vertical 9:16 (1080x1920) for Stories/Reels/TikTok and App Store preview. Horizontal 16:9 (1920x1080) for landing-page hero, Google Play preview. Square 1:1 (1080x1080) for Instagram feed. Animated WebP / GIF for fallback ambient loops <500KB. Lottie/Rive when the 'video' is actually vector animation (always prefer vector for UI animations).
file_size_performance: Web hero loop: target <1MB for 5s loop (use ffmpeg -crf 28, two-pass, mute audio if not needed). Mobile-loaded video: <2MB. App Store preview: <500MB upload, Apple compresses to ~5-15MB delivery. Always serve <video> with multiple sources (WebM + MP4 fallback) and poster image. Use video.muted + autoplay + playsinline + loop attributes for ambient hero loops to satisfy iOS autoplay policy.
free_tools: Google Veo 3.1 via Gemini Pro (USER HAS THIS — 8s 1080p, native audio, included in Gemini Pro subscription user already pays for; daily generation limits but not metered per-clip; some users report 60s mode for Pro — verify in Gemini app); Kling AI free tier (5 free generations/day, 5s 720p with watermark — solid for moodboarding); Luma Dream Machine free tier (~30 generations/month free, 5s clips); Pika free tier (limited credits on signup, 3-5 free clips/day); Runway Gen-3 Alpha Turbo free trial credits ($25 one-time on signup, ~125 seconds of video); Hailuo (MiniMax) free tier — surprisingly strong on motion physics, ~5 free/day; Krea.ai aggregator free tier with limited credits across multiple models; Hugging Face Spaces (free CogVideoX, Mochi 1, Wan 2.1 — slower but free unlimited); CapCut for editing/cutting (free desktop and web).
paid_tools: Veo 3.1 via Gemini API or Vertex AI (pay-per-second, ~$0.50/s); Kling Pro ($10/mo Standard, $26.99/mo Pro — best value for benchmark wins, native 4K); Runway Gen-4.5 ($15/mo Standard 625 credits, $35/mo Pro 2250 credits — best camera control); Pika 2.2 ($10/mo Standard, $35/mo Pro — best for 'Scene Ingredients' multi-element control); Luma Ray3 ($9.99/mo Standard, $29.99/mo Pro — solid HDR upscaling); Sora API (discontinuing April-September 2026 per OpenAI announcement — DO NOT BUILD ON). Tier guide: Starter $10/mo (Kling Pro or Pika Standard for one launch), Pro $25-35/mo (Runway Pro for camera control), Enterprise $50+ (multi-tool stack for agency work).
gemini_pro_workflow: User has Gemini Pro = user has Veo 3.1 + Imagen 4. This is the strongest single-bundle for AI video as of May 2026. Workflow: (1) Generate hero still in Imagen 4 — establish character/scene look. (2) Use that still as image-to-video reference for Veo 3.1 ('animate this scene with subtle camera dolly forward, ambient lighting shift'). (3) Use Gemini meta-prompting: 'Gemini, write 5 Veo prompts that animate this image like a Wes Anderson tracking shot but for a B2B SaaS landing page'. (4) Generate 8s clips in Veo (current ceiling — confirm 60s is unlocked for Pro tier). (5) Stitch multiple 8s clips in CapCut/DaVinci Resolve free for longer narrative. (6) Veo native audio means you skip royalty-free music search. Critical: Veo 3.1 prompt strategy is 'direct like a shot' not 'describe a scene' — use cinematography terms (dolly, push, tilt, rack focus, golden hour, 35mm).
workflow_steps: Step 1 (10 min): Define video purpose (hero loop / launch teaser / onboarding) and target spec (16:9 vs 9:16, duration). Step 2 (15 min): Storyboard 3-6 shots on paper or as a Figma row of frames. Step 3 (20 min): Generate hero still images in Imagen 4 / Gemini Pro for each shot. Step 4 (30 min): Image-to-video each frame in Veo 3.1 (8s each) — generate 2-3 variations per shot. Step 5 (20 min): Pick best clips, stitch in CapCut Free or DaVinci Resolve Free. Step 6 (15 min): Add captions (CapCut auto-captions free) — 80%+ of social viewers watch muted. Step 7 (10 min): Color-grade subtle (DaVinci has free color wheels) to break the AI default look. Step 8 (10 min): Export 1080p H.264 + WebM. Step 9 (5 min): Compress with HandBrake or ffmpeg to target file size. Step 10 (5 min): Drop on hosting (Mux, Bunny CDN, or just /public/hero.mp4). Total: ~2 hours for a polished 30s launch video, $0 if Gemini Pro covers it.
ai_prompt_templates: Veo 3.1 hero loop: 'Cinematic ambient shot: [scene] at [time of day], slow camera [push-in/dolly/orbit], [lighting style], [film stock reference], 8 seconds, no cuts, photorealistic, mood: [adjective1, adjective2]'. Veo 3.1 with character: 'Medium shot of [character description] [action], handheld camera, natural light, shallow depth of field, sound: [ambient]'. Image-to-video Runway Gen-4: 'animate this image with [camera move: dolly forward / orbit left / rack focus], maintain composition, subtle ambient motion'. Pika scene-ingredients: '[character.png] + [environment.png] + prompt: character walks toward camera, golden hour'. Universal counter-slop tail: 'no morphing, no melting, no jelly motion, stable subject, natural physics, no plastic skin, no slow-mo cliche'.
claude_code_integration: Claude Code can: (1) write ffmpeg scripts to compress / convert / loop / concatenate clips (e.g. seamless loop with crossfade); (2) script Veo / Runway API calls (generate batch from CSV of prompts); (3) generate <video> JSX with poster, multi-source, autoplay-safe attributes; (4) script captions extraction via Whisper for accessibility; (5) generate JSON schema describing each clip's prompt + output for reproducibility; (6) optimize hosting (push to Mux or Bunny via CLI); (7) generate App Store preview-video frame compositing scripts (overlay screen recording on top of AI-generated background).
emotional_design_integration: Camera language carries archetype. Caregiver: slow push-ins, warm golden hour, gentle handheld breathing — feels held/safe. Magician: dolly orbits, anamorphic lens flares, prismatic light, slow reveals — feels mystical. Hero: tracking shots, low-angle hero framing, dynamic motion — feels powerful. Jester: snap zooms, fast cuts, saturated palette, exaggerated motion — feels playful. Sage: locked-off static frames, neutral grade, geometric composition — feels authoritative. Lover: shallow depth of field, soft bokeh, intimate close-ups — feels romantic. Same scene of a phone-on-table — Caregiver gentle warm push-in vs Magician orbital reveal vs Hero crash-zoom — same content, three brands. Always specify camera move + grade in prompt to claim archetype.
specific_examples: Linear (linear.app) — landing-page hero combines real product motion with subtle ambient generative backdrop, Sage/Magician archetype. Vercel — uses kinetic generative loops on feature pages, Magician/Hero. Apple Vision Pro launch — heavy 3D + AI-assisted concept work, Magician/Hero hybrid. Volley AI — short-form character animation videos for marketing, Jester/Innocent. Krea.ai's own marketing — uses its own product to make demo loops, Magician archetype. App Store reference: search 'app preview video' on Smartmockups; for B2B reference Stripe, Linear, Vercel, Cursor.
anti_ai_slop_traps: AI video slop tells: (1) face/hand morphing across frames (eyes shift, fingers multiply); (2) jelly-motion physics (objects bend/stretch unnaturally); (3) lighting that 'breathes' (intensity flickers without source); (4) text on signs misspelled and shifting frame-to-frame; (5) generic 'cinematic' grade — the teal-orange Hollywood preset on every clip; (6) over-slow-motion default (everything is 60fps interpolated); (7) impossible camera moves (orbit + push + tilt + zoom simultaneously, no real cinematographer would do); (8) endless soft bokeh background blur; (9) flag/cloth physics melting. Counter-strategies: KEEP CHARACTER OFF-SCREEN or in silhouette/back-of-head shot if AI character consistency is unreliable; use product screen-capture for foreground and AI ONLY for backgrounds; constrain camera to ONE move per clip ('only push in, no other motion'); avoid text inside the video — overlay text in CapCut/DaVinci instead; specify natural physics ('cloth moves with gravity', 'liquid splashes naturally'); cut clips at 4-6s before AI artifacts compound. Hard NO list: do not use AI to fabricate fake user testimonials, fake faces speaking, or fake screen-recordings of features that don't exist (App Store rejection + ethical disaster).
common_pitfalls: (1) Trying long narrative — AI video can't hold a story across cuts, max ~8s coherence per clip, multi-clip stitching introduces continuity errors. (2) Character consistency — same character in 6 clips will subtly drift; use I2V from a single reference image to mitigate. (3) Exact text — AI cannot reliably render specific text; overlay text in post. (4) Sync to music — AI doesn't know your beat; cut to music in post. (5) Lip-sync for product testimonial — generally avoid; use real voiceover with B-roll. (6) File size — uncompressed AI 4K is huge; always re-encode for web. (7) Autoplay restrictions — iOS requires muted+playsinline; ensure poster image looks great because video may not auto-play on slow connections. (8) Motion sickness — heavy parallax loops can trigger reduced-motion users.
accessibility_notes: Always provide controls or set prefers-reduced-motion fallback (poster image instead of looping video). Captions mandatory for any video with speech (CapCut auto-caption is free). Avoid flashing >3Hz (epilepsy risk). aria-label on <video> if used as decorative. Provide a static poster fallback image with same emotional read.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile: vertical 9:16, lower resolution acceptable (1080x1920 max), heavy compression mandatory (cellular data), prefer Lottie/Rive over MP4 for short UI motions. App Store preview is strictly portrait device-frame, 15-30s, must show real app. Web: horizontal 16:9 hero, can serve 4K with adaptive streaming via Mux/Bunny CDN, autoplay-muted-loop is the standard hero pattern. Google Play preview videos are landscape 16:9, 30s-2min, hosted on YouTube. iOS Safari has stricter autoplay policy (must be muted + playsinline). Reduced-motion respect more critical on mobile.
sources: [Veo 3.1 vs Top AI Video Generators 2026](https://pxz.ai/blog/veo-31-vs-top-ai-video-generators-2026), [Best AI Video Generator 2026 — Pixflow](https://pixflow.net/blog/best-ai-video-generator/), [AI Video Bootcamp Ranking 2026](https://aivideobootcamp.com/blog/ai-video-generators-ranked-2026/), [Veo 3.1 Prompting Guide — Google Cloud](https://cloud.google.com/blog/products/ai-machine-learning/ultimate-prompting-guide-for-veo-3-1), [DeepMind Veo Prompt Guide](https://deepmind.google/models/veo/prompt-guide/), [Apple App Preview Specifications](https://developer.apple.com/help/app-store-connect/reference/app-preview-specifications/), [Google Play Preview Video Guidelines](https://www.gummicube.com/blog/aso-academy/google-play-store-preview-video-guidelines), [App Store Preview Video 2026 Conversion Guide](https://appscreenshotstudio.com/blog/app-store-preview-videos-the-2026-conversion-guide)
